Each staff member was amazing but i found Janet and Buffy to be particularly friendly and personable.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Daniel Douglas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWNUmqzx7C855tw_RqB1upZM2f3gW0a2daXph0nvsNNE3t7XZ5F=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Surgeon, phenomenal. Anesthesiologist, on point. OR nurses empathetic and doing their jobs properly, competent, plus friendly. The after care nurses empathetic and caring also, they actually answered the call button. The three stars are because the discharge nurse Madeline was subpar and uncaring. My wife pressed her call button at 6am, no nurse. Someone came into her room to turn off the beeping IV monitor because it was empty, then let my wife know that they will let her nurse know to come change out the IV bags. 10am, nurse Madeline rolls into the room. My wife asks her if she will be swapping out the IV bags. Madeline’s answer is no, because you will be discharged today. Keep in mind, my wife is not sure what day or time it is so she responds with “she thought she’d be discharged the next day”, to which Madeline instantly became irritated, saying she will go find out. Again, leaving an empty IV in my wife’s arm for yet another hour. Madeline returns saying she will be removing the IV line and discharging my wife even though my wife informed her that she had no one to pick her up as I was at work and there was no one home to let her into the house. Instead of showing empathy, Madeline insisted upon sending my wife home, after a major surgery, in an uber to sit outside and wait for me to get off of work. My wife called and texted me, worried, asking me when I was off work, I let her know, then moments later I receive a spoof call from the hospital nurse, no joke, the call came through as if it was from my job but it was the nurse asking me to come pick up my wife. Needless, to say, I had to leave work early to pick up my wife from the hospital as if she does not have great insurance, as if they could not wait for me to get off work, and as if they were bogged down with patient’s. Madeline made the experience horrible. My wife’s surgery started off pleasant but ended abhorrent thanks to Madeline being upset that she had to do her job, such as checking on a patient. I suggest for Madeline to find a new position, as nursing is not her forte.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tia G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLOEJy_-4rLFZqcZ85jpgY5wh19obPMZdY6o3h-sdpq0AD6GA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My experience at North Vista has always been a great place to work. What a wonderful team of Hero’s!!!!!!","Images":null,"When":""}]

The staff was professional, and thorough. The hospital was clean.  The food was healthy and decent. They have an incredible team.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJr48KleqE0eyhxV1_7HUEhzmmG9mQld9gUMgR6sPw239FttQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I went to Henderson Hospital ER and was registered 12/29/2024 at 4:51 PM. I was not discharged until 3am. 10 HOURS later. I believe I had blood work done within the first 2 hours. CT scan completed 7:25PM. After that I waited and waited granted there were a lot of people there. No one even followed up with me. By 2 AM I spoke with a Doctor. He said he was the 3rd doctor assigned to me but did not give me reasons why. He said the CT scan needed to be read by a third party company outside the hospital, that’s why it took hours. Someone should at least be monitoring those results and following up with the Radiologist. No patient should be hanging around the waiting area for results for 10 hours. I was prepared to wait for at least 5 hours but not 10 hours. I was sent home with an antibiotic prescription. It even took an hour to give me an IV shot of the antibiotic. A male nurse supposed to be doing the shot and discharge me after but I had to ask again what happened to him. Someone eventually called me to do the shot and discharge an hour later. I was also told I can access my CT Scan results online by scanning the QR code but it did not work as it said I need an invitation. Really, really bad experience at the Henderson Hospital ER specially if you are already sick. 10 hours!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chrisjen Walker","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXz7ZztHHTB8-_FjwgnC4EFQKJR-hbI2H0TsJxkSPQPq18gvbbi=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I recently visited Henderson Hospital and had mixed feelings about my experience. Every nurse, medical assistant, physician assistant, surgeon, surgical tech, and ultrasound technician I encountered was incredibly nice and made a genuine effort to ensure I felt comfortable throughout my visit. Their professionalism and compassion truly stood out, which helped ease the stress of the lengthy wait.

Overall, while the wait was inconvenient, the quality of care provided by the staff made a significant positive impact. I appreciate their hard work and dedication to patient well-being.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Truthy1","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIHp97GAuN5F7lsOR-5YJEtDaTPuIcf_oT1HhB35I4eOJSRTw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"These are not kind words, but a criticism of the performance of Henderson Hospital.  They need to do much better to properly serve the community.

Brought my girlfriend who had intense stomach pains.  She was left in a hall in a chair (no beds in emergency!). They did a CT scan which they said showed no problems.  They concluded it was gastritis and sent her home.  The next day the pains got more intense.  Brought her to St. Rose Martin.  She said they  took a much more comprehensive CT scan.  St. Rose  IMMEDIATELY found that she had appendicitis (the appendix was perforated).  I had asked specifically at Henderson Hospital if it could be the appendix and they said no!  She had surgery at St. Rose Martin by a very good surgeon.  Also, she had a far superior emergency room experience at St. Rose (excellent attention and a bed). I usually go to the Saint Rose Hospital on Eastern and Saint Rose Parkway. But that facility is always crowded. When I go to the ER at that hospital guaranteed weight of 2 to 3 hours. So I tried the other Saint Rose ER on the south west side of the valley near Rhodes Ranch. It is on warm Springs Road. This place is exceptional. It is clean and the emergency room never has a long wait. The doctors are fantastic and the nursing staff exceptional. I was also an inpatient at this hospital and I had the best nursing care ever. What I really like about this facility is that it is not terribly crowded. Usually you have no problems with parking or access. And the public cafeteria has very good sandwiches at an exceptional price. Just for a little context. The hospital has a small gift shop where you can buy flowers, stuffed toys and jewelry. It also has a cafeteria where you can get breakfast, lunch or dinner. They have a grill where you can get burgers, sandwiches, or full entrees.

And making jokes. “I can tell she’s not a fan of my jokes” The nurse wasn’t very helpful. I was ONE of two ER patients. When I asked her for something (not meds literally water) she proceed to tell me that another nurse will be in at 3 and he can help with that. (It was 2:15) She barely checked up on me meanwhile the DR checked up on me at least 7 times. I would never recommend coming here & had it been my choice I would’ve gone to summerlin or southern hills. Also who sends prescriptions to a CLOSED pharmacy? [{"Name":"Gabby Mendez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIxkSwvIV_DLujbhAYhrypRNCbc6YfJSI2nwdGm90MvgPH9pg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"The service I received was terrible I come in sick I have a bonnet on and the receptionist gives my paperwork and ask me what I got going on on my head?? First off I don’t feel good and don’t you think I already feel uncomfortable?? Thanks a lot! I feel out the paper work bring it back up to her and have a seat until they called my name. I get called back and the intake nurse did half of her job and acted as if she wasn’t happy to be at work I get back to the room and I’m told to wait on the Dr. this is the part that really bothered me is she ask me what brings me in I explain! I ask if I can get an IV because I feel weak and dehydrated she says no that there is a shortage on IVS and that I don’t look like I need one I then say I understand and she goes on trying to defend the fact that me drinking water and stuff is better then an IV. I’m sitting there and the receptionist comes back with the insurance paper work and stuff and gives me someone else paperwork an 18 year olds paperwork. I give her the paperwork back and tell her this is not mines she then gives me my clipboard with my paper work I sign what ever she needs and since I don’t got insurance I feel like I got treated as if I’m not enough do they know how hard it is out here especially when doing it alone Dr. Sinnott I don’t appreciate the way u treated me the lack of care and compassion and the fact that u gave me cough medicine instead or antibiotics for an upper respiratory infection is mind blowing I hope this comment gets to the right person and they take you back into training and remove your bias that you carry on you. What I’m trying to say is don’t judge a book by its cover you work in the medical field your job is to treat and make people feel better and that you didn’t do.
I will not recommend this place to anybody it’s not my first encounter I’m just now speaking out and please don’t get me wrong the nurse did a great job but the Dr was terrible.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Harmony Popa","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUd2nyIWvauPhfBhusYXJCh3rQRP79W9KDd1shBCP4czRURGKweDg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Unfortunately I landed in the ER here on 11/18/2024. I have a very high pain tolerance so for me to be here I was in severe pain. My best friend brought me in and immediately we were acknowledged by staff and security. My friend helped me fill out the paper work and before it was completed they had came out with a bracelet for me and my name was called within 5 minutes.

On the ER side they were quick, informative, respectful and kind of my situation. I had to do a urine culture, blood work, a CAT scan and a few other tests to determine what was wrong. Every person I encountered on the ER side had amazing bedside manner and spoke to me clearly and made sure I understood. It was told to me that I would need to be admitted to the hospital side due to signs of a kidney infection.

In the hospital side I was addressed by Astrid who explained things to me and what would be happening going forward. She was direct but extremely kind and patient. I had a room with 2 beds but it was only me in the room, it is a small hospital but very attentive and aware. The night nurses Lee and Talia took very great care of wore me. On 11/19/2024 I had nurse Lisa on the day shift and I enjoyed conversation with her as I was getting my
IV and antibiotics changed out.

I got fed 3 meals a day and the menu is very well put together with many options. Including those with diet restrictions. Even the ladies who brought me my food were kind and respectful and attentive. The hospital provided me with toiletries as I did not show up with anything.

Due to this being a small hospital their visiting hours are not so strict. I was now going on 2 days here and started to get lonely and a tad bit depressed from missing home and my every day life (no fault on their part) my family and friends were able to see me without any issues or pushback. This helped me feel better as I had those closest to me to help soothe my worries and concerns of me being here long.

11/20/2024: I visited the doctor via telemedicine in the ER which is convenient as I know since Covid it’s been hard to have drs anywhere due to the demand. I received a visit by the charge nurse David and the front end manager to see how my stay was and that made me feel like I was important and was being checked on genuinely. The fact that everyone was concerned about my care and asked to know if I was okay made me feel safe and cared about despite my condition and pain I was going through. This has been the most pleasant and welcoming hospital stay I have experienced and I’m glad this is where I chose to go to get care in my emergency.

Thank you, Dignity Health-Sahara Campus for your utmost care and attention to me while I needed help. It is truly appreciated. The kindness, respect, conversation and smiles will not be forgotten.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Shadow H","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXvZ5pUZIu3ILf2Hn0PHVsG9MsQuRR-27BUOt-czbJO4iAd8Flikg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Worst service ever my little sister came in due to falling on the back of her head and Crack it open and the front lady MARIA. !  didn't give a damm telling me I have to sit down and fill out paperwork and kept asking for insurance and only after freaking out and yelling for a doctor they came and helped. Don't come here if you have a choice.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Leslie Olson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUNT1NdBsbBIobQtClbJYeQzvWBZ8krZPZYI3yKrRdRR6wTQM-q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This used to be my go-to place for urgent care/emergency for myself and my children. My last visit was very disappointing. Check in is fast, but unfortunately so is the visit. I had a concern of swelling in my leg, which was clearly visible. The nurse was accommodating and listened to all of my concerns. Doctor, not so much. Did not even come in and examine me. Just looked at my vitals and discharged me with anxiety medication.

NEW: Don't take your children or babies here. I used to come to this place because it's a very short distance to my house and the closest 24 hour facility for emergencies. My one year old wasn't eating or swallowing. And constantly screaming. I was terrified. Yet, they could not and would not give us any answers. These people need to go back to school. She had HFM.

Car accident around the same time and my whole family was seen. Did not even put the effort with the responsible insurance company. Now they want us to pay out of pocket? Because they can't do their job correctly?

I will never return to this establishment. Taking my healthcare needs to UMC from now own. Please don't ever come here. None of the staff care about people. Doctors, nurses, office. Nobody cares and you will not get the help you need. It's all a scam.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Billy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIAatc_nyIxGKPTjzEhpVoqaAFVKHYSYSlDBuBW9nxJZeVdRQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great hospital!! The staff was very polite and extremely helpful! Highly recommend it!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Geo K","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWXOzVpPzQ5byHawuKNnudS3K2wlN1zu7OHEobYjgAbq9blc-oXFQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Competent, friendly, helpful staff, quick ER visit for an injury while on vacation, would definitely recommend this place, as it was recommend to us by a friend in the healthcare field!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rick McLaughlin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVrfK0DNvlp8OxsqXiBeW9PqaThtZuWYqfXPt3Et6yGvLUc-sRi=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Currently attending physical therapy sessions here and the staff is very accommodating and do their utmost best to ensure a good pain-free session! The physical therapy was not discussed (except by me) and  I was treated with respect and dignity by the physical therapy staff and the hospital staff, throughout. KUDOs to Dignity Health for going above and beyond. You'll always be in my thoughts!!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Carlos Sanchez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXa3NsbeSA-u-sbMDH6GY9LFHIN7UFeOWkvY3lN1kJmSK6j97np=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I was charged $27237.00 for an emergency room visit for a kidney stone. [{"Name":"Robert Hutter","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXB3PVsnex4DvQgdYLogzS52t7meo37MfnXjm02SMQH5tD2SlUg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently spent 4 nights and 5 days at this hospital after breaking my kneecap, and I can’t thank the staff enough for their exceptional care. The rooms were a pleasant surprise—very clean, modern, and comfortable. Having my own private room made the recovery process much easier.

Overall, my experience at this hospital was as positive as it could be given the circumstances. I’m truly grateful for the excellent care I received and would highly recommend this hospital to anyone in need of medical attention.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lloyd Johnson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKNUaTs8fXeLROHAEaztmTwWQ8XtnqwIUizAxPfUdobuTEFdg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I rate the services, medical care and overall care with a Ten. Every staff I encounter was knowledgeable, friendly and professional. I had several blood draws and EVERYONE of them was painless. If a child would be delighted. I don't want to try and list staff by name and accidentally miss one. I felt the human kindness and love. WONDERFUL service. Thanks","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Margarette Campos","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocItpXiIDozsxunG2nWofxm5X07PEXBehAy24-P1vWeBFcSA7Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Checked in in ER at 12:30 pm. I have waited maybe just an hour and a nurse already attended to me check My vitals and the doctor already told me the care they will do. I was move to the ER observation area inside and was assigned to an ER RN, when she saw my concern she said “you can’t pull it off yourself?” Shocked by her comment I went completely silent, I already knew Im given to a nurse that have been dried up from years of work - but still completely unnecessary to say that to a patient that went to ER to ask for help. I waited an hour before I pressed on the call button  I asked her if she could give me something for pain or ask the doctor to put an order one for me so that can help relieved  pain for at least the waiting period as I already anticipated that it could be a long wait since my case is not a life and death situation. But her answer was automatically why you need pain reliever for? Doctor will just inject numbing medicine and you won’t feel a thing - you do not need one! As a nurse, One must understand everyone has different pain tolerance. I am honestly taken aback by how her nursing care is. Maybe 2-3 times I tried to ask for help for my nurse but it’s always a different nurse that checks me out even though I can actually see and hear my nurse across from the observation room I was in, talking about previous patients she handled while she talks and talks and talks with some other nurse about some “foley” or whatever! That is completely unacceptable!  that is a HIPPA violation! while completely ignoring the call bell. Doctor came at 1500 injected the area that needs to be numb and told me it’s going to kick in 15-20 mins but wasn’t able to come back til 1640, the local already wore off - although I totally understand ER needs to prioritize, there might be more critical patients the doctor needs to attend to. But this nurse completely ignores my call for help purposely, between the times 1530-1630 she completely ignores I was already crying and she  have not shown any compassion at all, she’s not able to check in the patients she’s assigned to and just keep on gossiping LOUDLY on previous patients she handled. A nurse that wasn’t assigned to me willingly came up and ask for what helped I need and even suggested to talk to the charge nurse. I honestly regret now not talking to the charge nurse as offered by the guy nurse who I vented out to. I should’ve brought up my concern, but at that moment I felt sympathy on her as we are all just humans. But thinking about it now, I’m disgusted by how this “lead nurse” Handled the situation - it seems to, as my experience she already lost her nursing heart. Entirely lost what nurses should be and what this hospital/institution motives and goals. It saddens me that the core meaning of  her profession which is “caring” is gone gone GONE. Other than the CEUs or learning courses that all hospital’s require healthcare workers/ nurses to do, maybe maybe THEY NEED TO CHECK AND REMIND BACK NURSES TO BE MORE HUMAN.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipNhZgzQIwxvYqgqRqFITcbcweAIknT_W0Q4dOt-&fid=0x0:0x1bbed9b0699f6a3f"],"When":"2024-7-6"},{"Name":"Mercedes Dior","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU8EcUZBboihgG6PV8DF4NSzke3-HzX5nq7Yb4GpV_7QGqffD7jfw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Worst hospital to ever exist ! My son went in due to a fever and heavy breathing . As we arrive the nurses ended up pokeing my son 7 times by 3 different doctors trying to get a IV in and all 7 times they failed . Leaving my son with marks and bruises . After being in the  hospital for 9 hours we were told we need to transfer to a pediatrician hospital. My 2 year old was diagnosed with broncolitis and Pneumonia before we were transferred by the ambulance . We have arrived at dignity health and they ran quick tests and said he was fine and sent us home . I'll never again go to this hospital again","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"mike sayegh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJqymfLbCsq18gEdbzv6ria_5LjAi-ED5CDCciSaYNDs1BkLw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The staff here at Dignity health we have a rehab rehabilitation hospital is fantastic. Also, the food here is the best food that I’ve ever had in the hospital. I actually have dietitian come to your room and gives you all kinds of choices for food which is good and I’ve been here a little over two weeks and I’m going home on Tuesday, but I’m very satisfied with my progress and hopefully this time I keep up with my regimen like I’m supposed to Very clean facility nice facility As close to my home if you’re gonna be sick, this is a place to be sick","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Elizabeth Allen","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ3uzlPCfYAkBJCq1QEbXab42SdUXXDG7pcAdWD0AVj18iI9Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I came into the emergency room waiting room Tuesday evening, sent there with a high fever, straight from my primary care doctor's office.

By the time I got to the emergency room check-in line I could hardly breathe and was rushed straight to the back, turns out I had a
Bilateral pneumonia and a fever of 103°, my heart rate was 150+.

St Rose Dominican isn't even Networked with my insurance, but because I was so gravely ill, once my labs came back the ER doctor requested approval from my insurance and I was admitted to the hospital right away.

I was released Friday night after days of extensive antibiotics and heart monitoring and am now at home recovering with prescription medications, feeling 99% better then the day I walked into this place!

I am impatient at times I felt care was slow or I wasn't being released fast enough or something didn't make sense to me, but hey I have no medical education and I am home with my children and feeling better today so I am so grateful for the quality care I received that made this all possible!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Diamond D","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI1Q3pxtthQYM_17Wui-3YzKwa8EWSbLnVqhZ5UIB2VkkEiJA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I just had my 2nd baby here. I love this hospital. The labor and delivery team is amazing.. they are thoughtful, they listen and they don’t pressure me to do anything I don’t desire to do. If you’re having a baby, come to this hospital.. Hands down the best place to have a baby In Vegas. Thank you guys for being amazing In a very vulnerable time.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"shea irwin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKfE6yh8HK9Nr3lmCPeJxHq19-Fanq8IuA0vvP0ZNOa7wHtIw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My first knee surgery, My bed was broken, so I was unable to elevate my leg. My chair was broken, so I couldn't lower my legs without a lot of help. My first morning, for PT, the young person was too captivated by the TV to pay attention, ignored my cries for help,and allowed my leg to drop,bending my knee without support.  I screamed in agony, and he ran away,never to be seen again.  That,set me back in my recovery.  I don't know how far,but I couldn't move for a week.  My surgeon is a genius, I'm doing fantastic, but my experience was an example of medieval torture. My second knee? 15 days ago? That was a fantastic experience.   The nursing staff,for both knees were absolutely fantastic. Those people are angels, without their empathy, I would not have been able to do both knees.All my nurses,were the best of humanity, absolute perfection.","Images":null,"When":""}]

Everyone from housekeepers to administration were kind to my wife and I.  My wife has been sick for a while and been in and of hospitals here in Las Vegas. The care here was some of the best we have had.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Eduany Alvarez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI3Fq2MtnvXSkbwQR9MbApvHcnCXNFAeTRtYO7NuUWnGya_gQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It's the best LTAC in town.
“Save one person; you’re a hero. Save hundreds, you’re a nurse.”

I am forever thankful for the care my grandfather received at AMG Las Vegas, Nevada. A few weeks ago, our lives changed when our lovely grandfather got critically ill, and his condition was deteriorating by the day. After weeks in Henderson Hospital, we had to make a difficult decision to look for a Long Term Acute Care facility where he could receive the best care to recover after a stroke successfully. With so many choices in town, we toured some of the recommended facilities, but nothing felt right until we came to AMG in McLean. From the moment we walked in, we were treated like family. The building feels like home, and the staff are the most professional and compassionate we have met in town. My grandfather spent almost two months there. We greatly compliment the nurses, CNAs, Respiratory therapists, physical therapists, occupational therapists, and the entire team. Contradictory to other LTACs in town, at AMG, the nurses have fewer patients assigned to them; therefore, they have more time to treat each patient like family. The building itself is kept clean at all times. Each room has huge windows, which, in my opinion, make my grandpa feel like he was at home. We could visit him at any time and stay in if we wanted; there are no restrictions on the visitation hours. By the time Grandpa was transferred there, the doctors at Henderson didn't promise he would be able to breathe on his own; nevertheless, the tireless effort of the professionals at AMG achieved the impossible: Grandpa could be disconnected from the vent in less than two weeks. The leaders at AMG even gave us their cell phone numbers so we could call them anytime if we had any issues. The nurses always took time to update us on Grandpa’s condition, as well as to explain everything they were doing for him. We are forever thankful for this place. Overall, this LTAC is quiet, clean, well-organized, and has the most amazing healthcare professionals in town. We genuinely believe that our Grandpa has fully recovered because of the fantastic care he received at AMG OF Las Vegas.","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"S. Bursey","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKECCThc0UEVXk8XhPmb-FdtVrn9Jq3Yk8U13kVNyqKg-u8S6w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"My grandmother was "sentenced" to stay here and saying she was mistreated is a gross understatement. The staff was incredibly rude and disrespectful.. .. just a few examples:
-staff would routinely enter her room and not say a single word, she would say hello and no response
-staff would give her medication and she would as what is this for, many times they replied with smart comments or simply they didn't know just take it
-staff on several occasions would bring her food in the room place it on the tray far away from her and she could not reach it and she would begin asking them for assistance in bringing the tray closer and the staff would ignore her and proceed to walk out of the room
The CNA's, nurses and all support staff are incompetent and make no excuses for their lack of knowledge and have an I don't care attitude.
I've never in life seen anything like this circus, one of the "leaders" Jean, is a rude, bitter and uneducated woman, she should not be working with the public, I honestly wouldn't allow her to dog sit my neighbor’s dog.  The permanent frown on her face tells you that she does not want to be there or be bothered. They answer the phones laughing and make smart comments, hang up on you, cut you off while you are speaking, place you on hold mid-sentence.
Not being harsh just a bunch of rejects. The entire time my grandmother was there I DID NOT encounter ONE nice staff member.
The attitudes are one thing to deal with but their lack of concern for their patients is unbelievable! My grandmother got bed sores, and staff did nothing, not one bit of sympathy even, no concern.  I won't go into details but the entire way of doing things here is unsanitary.
I will NEVER allow ANY family member or friend or anyone I know to receive care here. One star is more than what they deserve.
PLEASE DO NOT ALLOW YOUR LOVED ONE TO BE MISTREATED HERE!!!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kimberly Scott","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJoZ7XNXqygyEdS2k-m12Ohl2QFv3BZxJpAmQTHlv4VZg2BoA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Worst Care facility I have ever seen! If I could give them a zero I would. They are understaffed, not enough CNA'S or Nurses. The staff have the worst attitude I ever seen. They lack compassion for their patients. If your loved one is going to survive, you better have a family member or close friend there 24/7. My family was there all day every day! We saw the extremely poor care other patients were getting, and even the poor care they tried to give my mother. However, we advocated for her, and refuse to let them get away with improper practices. HERE ARE JUST A FEW EXAMPLES: They gave her the wrong meds the first day she was there; they ran out of clean linen over the weekend, and I had go to Walmart to purchase blankets; the  back door,  near the alley way was not locked. Anybody could come in at any time. I complained several times and they finally fixed it. The worst was their inability to take care of my mom's ostomy bag (she had just had surgery and the ostomy was new). They did not have the ostomy products she needed, so I had to go to Valley hospital at 9:00 PM to ask them if we could borrow some. The nurse put it on wrong and it was leaking by morning. I had to bring the extra bags Valley Hospital gave me to use. My family was devastated by what we endured there! The CEO, Robin Hager, and the Director of Nursing, Jackie Nissen met with me and my husband, and tried to insinuate that I was causing problems, because I was speaking up about the care Mom was receiving. After one week we moved my Mother to Advanced Health Care, which is right down the street from this place, and it was like night and day. A completely different experience! They are so professional and the facility is AMAZING! I am filing a complaint on Complex Care/PAM Hospital, or whatever they are calling themselves these day! DO NOT TAKE YOUR LOVE ONES TO THIS PLACE, IT IS A NIGHTMARE!!!!!!!!!! By the way, Robin Hager,  the CEO, called me to ask me for the receipts for purchases I made, they wanted to reimburse me for the items I had to buy, that they should have provided.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Ben Gugled","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWmhlzXHhAa6vep6shwOJ1NIHPPvWazqchTZop5mheXnHoQJ1W4=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"My mother was suffering from liver failure and was sent here for long term care, she was in really poor condition and needed constant care, she would not eat and required a lot of patience to get her to consume even a small amount of food, same with her medications. We arrived to find a cold tray of food  next to her and her medications  left out by the tv stand. We ended up having to be there 3 times daily to make sure she ate and was medicated properly. She ended up needing a liver transplant and was rushed to UCLA medical center to wait for a donor, upon arrival they found she had a serious blood infection and started treatment ( when I was there I observed a nurse change the IV bag without wearing gloves and asked her about it she stated that there would be no issues ) They stated that she was stable and could very soon be sent home.  Be very careful with your loved ones if they are sent here. Horrible service and care, all the Filipina nurses sit around and chat, if you request anything they take their time helping you and even  not doing anything will still take their time. Our mother received a transplant and is getting better each day and we are thankful for this. Just wanted to let you know our experience.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sheila Swenson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKzoNawlWjjxLjssrj_Q2U-Z-LsAmfk2wcDpL4M11gaLXSz2g=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My mom has been to a few places and I have to say this place has treated her very good. They are very caring, helpful & knowledgeable about the care that they give at Complex Care. I am a very protective daughter to my mother who has had a stroke and very into her recovery. Complex Care is one of the best places here in the Northwest part of town.  If your loved one needs care please check this place out.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sharonda newman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWQ6cFY9UStzy8uJLYwckN6V-OjcnwAUrmq_YqCnGHXzi0-YHvk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"If you would like your family member to recover and not DIE than this isn’t the facility for you. PLEASE FOR YOUR LOVED ONES SAKE DONT!!!

This facility is understaffed. They have to many patients and not enough CNA’s, nurses, and doctors. Meaning they are unable to carry out a high standard of care.
One example: When my mother was a patient there when we would call of FaceTime (due to Covid) specific cares she would need would NEVER be met in a timely fashion. Literally my mother colostomy bag popped and she was laying in a soiled for extended amount of time we had to call the facility 3 times just to get someone to her room to help.

She suffered bed sores, which anyone who has worked in healthcare knows are from lack or care, and rotation.

The staff is unprofessional. Example the nurse (Joyce) told us she “can’t stand the smell of my mothers room” “it smells like someone is dying” “you should look into hospice, I told your mother that as well” “when I go in the room I feel like throwing up”
Are you serious!!! And during Covid when we can’t even enter the property to be there for our loved one these are the conversations we have with the staff.

We called everyday and it wasn’t until continuous complaints from my mother that we actually demanded to speak to the doctors which are rarely available, to hear them state that they don’t think your mother is understanding everything yet do not even try to reach out to the family to discuss medical concerns. The doctor who I won’t name who was honest said “it’s sad that they don’t have enough staff, the patient to cna ratio is like 12:1” Joyce the RN upon my mothers transfer from their facility to MV ICU advised that we do “contact administration to file complaints for the lack of communication”

Unfortunately, 6/28/20 we FaceTimed with mother for over an hour, which led us to a long conversation with doctor on 6/29/20 discussing all her ailments and plans to increase care, to later that evening Heather calling asking about discharging my mother to a nursing facility. To 6/30/20 doctor calling to say my mother had trouble breathing last night, to 7/1/2020 655am your mother “stopped breathing this morning, they came in the room, THEY DONT KNOW HOW LONG SHE WAS UNCONSCIOUS FOR” CPR was given her heart is beating. 721am Joyce RN calls states “she needs meds to keep blood pressure up to keep heart beating”” I told you to look into hospice””yes there was a lot of lack in communication” but she’s stable. 742am Joyce RN calls again “ you need to make a decision!” “We can’t keep giving CPR” “she is suspicious of Covid” “ we are all being put as risk even being in there” “what do you want to do” we reply keep fighting keep our mother’s heart beating, while on the call the doctor notify her that she is stable we are transferring her to Mountain View hospital.

So what decision were you trying to force us to make. Suspicious of Covid when she came there Covid free just to recover from a surgery on her stomach? My mother everytime we called would say she was in pain, she would say she felt like a guinea pig being there. I wish I would of got her out sooner....

Maybe she would be Alive 7/1/2020 at 1029am she died at MV hospital after her stay at PAM facility for 4 weeks at the young age of 66.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lulu Penalosa","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVKFlj3dWoiMKV-5lphM7wCdMrjZTbaFCQPr6AvGJwYwK86Z3Zs=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We want to Thank You for taking good care of my husband, to recover from Covid-19, Dr Victoria, Patricia, Ebony, Luna, Fernando, Piper, Chris, Tyler and the respiratory team. From our family THANK YOU SO MUCH.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lashunda Austin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIVfYfZESQWIHwy05hjmmKBS4KIecZWM8Q6FwZQPakbfxzfmg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This is a horrible place. He had never seem her or anything but whenot our family asked to speak with him the Charge RN Anna claim there's no doctors or physical therapist  until Monday later to find out they were unable to care for her and she had to be transferred back to the hospital were her body almost went into shock due to the lack of care. Endangering lives of this community on a daily basis.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tiffany Wilson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUUcZBe270cgk4pjIpGUQEgjz1xtwqWw59g_52hvbV2zUm4aqrD=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I am writing this review out of deep concern and frustration regarding my mother's treatment and the overall communication at Desert Parkway Behavioral Health Facility. My mother was admitted to this facility in a non-coherent state and experiencing severe hallucinations, and the lack of clear communication from the staff has been alarming.

Initially, there was no explanation provided when my mother was abruptly transported to Sunrise Hospital. This sudden transfer without any prior notification or explanation left our family in a state of confusion and anxiety. To make matters worse, after her brief stay at Sunrise Hospital, she was sent back to Desert Parkway without any clear communication about her condition or the reasons behind these transfers.

Since her return, it has been three days with virtually no communication from the facility. Despite her initial state of severe disorientation, the limited updates we receive are contradictory, with staff claiming that she is talking and fine. This is in stark contrast to the condition she was in when she was first admitted. The inconsistency in these updates has made it incredibly difficult for us to understand her current state and the steps being taken for her recovery.

Moreover, there seems to be no concrete plan communicated to us regarding her future treatment or how the facility intends to address her current problems. As a family member, this lack of transparency and communication is unacceptable and deeply concerning.

Overall, the experience with Desert Parkway Behavioral Health Facility has been incredibly distressing due to the poor communication and lack of clear treatment plans. I urge the management to improve their communication protocols and provide families with the necessary information and reassurance during such critical times.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kristen Jackson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWAxssw9P-y4wSUzG2HS8xFOGn5eY3hQ0baxG0wfqxAWzBIk37d=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I did both inpatient and partial hospitalization here and it was honestly pretty helpful. Inpatient was as comfortable as it could be mostly. I've heard some horror stories so I was afraid to come in, but the staff was mostly quite kind and accommodating. They largely understood my needs.
For the partial hospitalization it is a lot of group therapy and processing. The staff has been very helpful and informative. You can tell they care, which is nice.
Overall, I'd recommend this place if you're struggling and need help. The main thing is to keep and open mind and remember that the therapy and experience is what you make of it. Engage in the programs, be serious about your goals and trying to improve your mental health or addiction issues and, from my experience, you'll get the help you need.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marie K Cabanit","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLVvjT5xScd5x-tVmyVR_HXEYaSU63AiqURIEwoOdhiACYnOA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I am so very grateful for this place for taking very good care of my daughter from inpatient to outpatient care you guys are so amazing!!! I’d see one neurologist in the community to do the same thing as three different departments here. Also, I’ve been waiting for more than 2 hours for an appointment that I showed up early for.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipPxYmTDfDJqF_F8YjWdGEPo8etNcn-pm1q_5Qw4&fid=0x0:0x9f2b34a5dd5b4845"],"When":"2024-9-13"},{"Name":"Mr Polo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW1HfU3VZ-cYevvUxjWNzgMcKZ29GK-u5ueEEMONVSt2-AHPR1Xsw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"08/09/2024. And 9/30/24

A big Salute to Dr Robt-Marlo Bautista MD AND his 3 nurses Alexis LPN, Michele RN, Cathy Navigator nursery and Laura RN. Whether on the phone or in person they all treated me with respect and dignity & Dr Bautista will be my future surgeon using the Da Vinci robotic for my hernia repair.
9/30/2024 Returned for 2 week post operative follow up and came in to talk to Dr. Bautista again who is a young, energetic, kind, compassionate caring special individual. He holds the rank of Captain and takes good care of his health no drinking or smoking and exercises regularly. He also takes care of emergency surgeries that come into the VA HOSPITAL. He did a normal job on the robotic Surgery called the Da Vinci machine. my follow up exam went great and looked great, and I even wore my navy uniform and hat, and made sure that when I saw him this time, I saluted him because I wanted to honor his skill set and taking care of me. Again, God bless the doctor and his staff, and I think the hospital for, such excellent care.
They really pay attention to cleanliness and sanitation . they had me bathe and washed with a special soap the night before surgery in the morning before and when I arrived, they gave me six more antiseptic towels for my arms, legs, front and back. They gave me his special mouthwash, toothbrush, and even iodine swabs to swab out, my nostrils. I was impressed because I know myself that bacteria and infections can really be life changing and detrimental. Should you acquire it during or after surgery. The packet they give you is very detailed and makes it important that you keep your incisions, clean and dry and don’t bathe and take showers or lift weights anymore than 10 pounds for six weeks afterwards.

I also want to mention and Salute Pre-Ops Staff Nurse Practitioner Ronnie Bowen who took a lengthy time with me going over bloodwork, chest X-rays and EKG TRACINGS. He was awesome. His nurse helper Rochelle .Great personal attention. Thank you for Kentesha at the front desk who’s always smiling as she greets all us vets.

A salute to the X-Ray staff Duncan Johnston who was excellent and really great personality. Made me feel very comfortable.

Also a special special thanks for the anesthesiologist and I’m sorry I miss placed his name. His job is very important to bring you back after they put you out before surgery.

The Optical department Allana Fa Garagan great as she found my lost glasses that were missing for months and the always kind desk receptionists always kind and patient all day long or after hour with lines of patients and telephone calls. I have to answer. Sorry, I forgot their names.

The phlebotomist   Alejandro in the lab was expedient and wonderful as well as beautiful black lady who I forgot her name was also great two days later when I went back to the lab.

Thanks to the entire VA Hospital personnel from top to bottom and all the vendors and volunteers who make the hospital run like a well oiled clock.

Jesus cares say your prayers. Amen!
*******************************************

Dear Radiology and staff personnel.

I visited late yesterday afternoon and was met by Britney at the counter and a radiologist named Giovanni. They were both excellent and I had great rapport with them. They both had smiling faces and I could see loved working with people.

Thanks so much for a wonderful visit to Radiology.

Sincerely, Marc Mercury, Veteran Seabee NMCB-40. 68/69.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipOVxU4Xls_4FXcuvEod2hx0spGGxLNKdivu2yCp&fid=0x0:0x9f2b34a5dd5b4845"],"When":"2023-4-19"},{"Name":"Lonnie Lilly","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWBUTmRBjyB_K0c_6SJ81tL06OUi2bkVNoP5HyY7CNwJbpCUcUM0w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Not sure how this place has 4 stars. And I’m not sure why I even continue to come here hoping to get help. It’s useless as a young back woman. I’m over it.
Doctors rush you out with no answers. Even with MRI results or x-rays that say other than “you’re okay”.
Bad mouth patients and other staff members right in front of patients.
I seen a Dr. Yesterday, that told me straight up “I’m sorry that you are not getting the proper care that you need and deserve, and I hate to say it, but it’s because you are black”. I was shocked that she was of other race and noticed it without me even saying it… I am tired, sick, and frustrated with VA medical. They really shouldn’t treat people this way. Especially, after serving this country. Perhaps this is why so may suffer in silence.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Jakob Zetwick","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVZC9D5MDqatPB-UvRCAKLopiUT--KFHQWB-LRL1WdgxvGGtMhB=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"It might be a clean and maintained building, but the right hand doesn’t talk to the left hand here. Much like all VA facilities, do not expect these people to adequately guide you through the steps you need to take in order to get adequate care. Always remember to advocate for yourself. Ask clarifying questions, and do not settle for subpar service.
There are a few here that do care but sadly, it seems the majority are here for a paycheck so quality is hard to come by. Most of the time I opt for community care, and that has served me well so far.
Along with all of that, there are times parking can be a pain here so when you do have appointments here, arrive a bit early.","Images":null,"When":""}]

I went right to Southern Hills Hospital and they found stones this was in the same day only 1 hour after leaving this bad hospital","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Meserakeg G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXtsv1vTFqcLmTgNv-22inq8O61d17J0-nZgwD6fmF4GhvyThA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Took my mother in to the ER on Thanksgiving evening. Front desk staff were great and it was what we thought was a good start to a rough evening. A few issues we experienced:
1. First person to come in to the room was a “tech” as it said on his name badge. For some reason he was the first to come to the room and ask my mother questions and immediately stated that she wasn’t well due to not taking medication daily. He was not personable or friendly, spoke almost in a condescending and cold manner was very matter of fact in his “diagnosis” before even knowing all of the symptoms or how the medication was prescribed to her. He also kept looking at my 6-yr old daughter in an almost uncomfortable and in un-approving manner which I had no understanding of he seemed bothered that she was there, minding her business on her iPad. When my mother asked me if I noticed it too I was taken aback.

2. The emergency room doctor finally arrived and while speaking to my mother about symptoms and health history, she proceeded to write all of this on her GLOVE! Flipped her palm over and continued to write on the same glove on the back of her hand?!?! Are you kidding me?

3. Once the doctor left, the previous tech and another one came in and while he was preparing my mother for her test, he and the other nurse tech and talking about the current ER doctor and the oncoming doctor and how needy they were and how they didn’t like working with them, basically “gossiping” about the doctors and having personal conversations in the hospital room all while somewhat working on my mother.
They all took our kindness for weakness and made assumptions about how they could behave in front of us based on our appearance and silence (it was complete shock).
People come in, in a vulnerable state and expect dignity and respect, which they obviously take full advantage of. They were disrespectful, unprofessional and abhorrently oblivious to their job and the profession they’ve selected. Disgusted. I waited to write a review to see if my disdain would simmer, but no. I’m just as pissed and will shout this from the rooftops if asked about this location.","Images":null,"When":""}]

SHAME ON YOU","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Richard Armah","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIGGStjMnRFfVpDVvvHB257rqr0PgfathY3QxPy28nZhMYrBZY=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Our experience at Nellis Airforce Base Hospital during my wife's hernia surgery was less than satisfactory. This was our second time at the facility, and the hernia had worsened since her first surgery in July 2023, necessitating the use of a mesh.

However, our concern primarily revolved around the recovery period. We requested a more extended rest period, given the physically demanding nature of my wife's job, but the surgeon was dismissive and made a remark that lacked empathy. Dr. (Major) Matthew Schreiner's comment, "nobody asked her to do the surgery," left us questioning the professionalism and care provided by the surgeon. Trust is a critical element in healthcare, and this experience left us uncertain about the level of care my wife would receive. I honestly will not recommend Mike O’Callaghan Military Hospital.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Adrienne Stone","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUYff6C-l55VTpx7FDpb9ZWoZY0wN_RawOxQeLlXjQ8ge7kDtrd=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"For the last three years, I have been having ongoing issues regarding my menstrual cycles. However, I have been actively prevented from seeking help from the Nellis Women's Health office regarding these issues. The Women's Health office regularly changes its policies from  only seeing active duty to not seeing anyone to seeing dependents only in certain circumstances. There is no consistency. Kim at the Women's Health front desk will not even allow me to make an appointment to speak with a gynecologist about my cycle. When I made an appointment for a PAP, the doctor who did my exam told me she is not allowed to discuss anything other than the PAP, so I could not tell her about my concerns regarding my cycle. FMR will not write a referral and wants to treat my cycle concerns in the FMR office, which is unacceptable as they are not specialists. I have requested an off- base referral from FMR twice and been denied both times. Due to these issues specifically, my husband opted to purchase supplemental insurance through his employer so that I could see a gynecologist off base without a referral. I find it to be a disgrace and a disservice to your veterans that a retiree must pay out of pocket for separate insurance, not because care isn't available, but simply because the medical facility with the means to provide gynecological services willfully refuses to provide said services. The fact that the doctor who did my PAP is not permitted to speak freely with her patients is appalling and goes against every medical principal that I am aware of. I am now being seen off base for my illness due to the reckless denial of services by the Mike O'Callaghan Medical Center. Veterans and their families deserve better Women's Health care than what Nellis AFB is providing to us.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Willow B.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVcsTmTdxGQrYASpowR1koyYivcLmXloVW8giGIZ4X712PAaoMpyw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"After my careful consideration, I have decided to shed light on the behavior of the medical staff here at Nellis. I've heard rumors of poor care, unprofessional behavior, and biased charting. My recent appointment has enlightened me on which individuals could be contributing to the negative reputation of the medical facilities on base.

During my visit to follow up on blood labs for an iron deficiency, I had an interaction with a medical technician. Initially, the technician appeared friendly and asked the standard questions, including any dietary changes. I openly shared the foods I had integrated into my diet. However, as soon as the technician left the room, the entire atmosphere changed abruptly. To my shock, I overheard her openly mocking the information I had shared, going so far as to imitate my voice when discussing my diet. This incident left me feeling both stunned and confused, as our initial interaction had been pleasant enough, and I couldn't comprehend why my words were being ridiculed.

I understand that humor can be a coping mechanism in the medical field, and I have friends who work in similar settings. However, they possess the professionalism and discretion not to make jokes at the expense of patients, and certainly not in a loud or disrespectful manner. It certainly raises the question of how many patients have been subjected to similar experiences.

By sharing my negative experience, I really hope that positive changes can be enacted. Life isn't easy, and we should all be kind and empathetic to one another.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"alex turner","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKBsxPn415dObWcFxcob36vs7MLKjpP-QtUNN1zkDUP3n3KfA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I went to TOPA to inprocess and waited 30 minutes to get my wife and kids a PCM. The tech told us they don’t do that in person anymore and that I’d have to do that on “the app.”

I called the pediatrics clinic to see if I could get some help. A tech called me back and asked what the situation is. I explained and they said they’d work it out with TOPA and would call me back. It’s been 3 days and no call back.

They have all sorts of different varieties of specialists here.   From general primary care to kidney specialists, oncologists, physical therapy , acupuncture, mental health doctors, nutritionist, dentists, and  lots of other kinds of care programs and doctors that they offer here.  They do have an emergency room as well.   I dont know why places dont like to use parking garages to save on space but, at least at this place its covered parking for the most part that so happens to have solar panels on top which is good.    Sometimes they have valet, sometimes they don't.   Of course they have handicap spots. It's a little funny though if you think about it because most, if not half of the people who come here are handicapped anyways. I’d totally recommend this hospital to anyone looking for good care! ❤️💯","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Dont Trip","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLduxciZtUTR_D7Rkq9Vd9xerooy3Mb1pphpqng25Gns7Cb6Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The day shift are lovely can't speak for all but the ones that helped my dad definitely did care but sadly the overnight really need to be monitored or atleast get a therapist or something they're very rude to patients and seem to do unnecessary things because they have the power to","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lani F","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKmsRC3H6t0qcREoy2PmR6VPbYeOUEJCG6P26GDqvnBCnGTlw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Writing this review has been difficult for me, but I hope that sharing my experience can help others make better choices. My sister's stay at Horizon was marked by more downfalls than ups, leading to a deep sense of disappointment.

The care and oversight at this facility seemed alarmingly inadequate. My sister, who has a history of brain bleed, managed to dislodge her feeding tube and trach on multiple occasions and even fell once. This, I'm convinced, has worsened her cognitive health. Instead of getting a clear explanation, the accounts I received were misleading and didn't align with my sister's apparent bruises.

Most of the staff at Horizon didn't meet my expectations. One CNA, Whitney, was particularly harsh with my sister. It seemed as though she had her preferred patients, and if you weren't one of them, she was noticeably hostile. I frequently noticed blood in the suctioning cup from my sister’s trach, a sight that was especially common after Whitney's shifts. This is simply unacceptable. Then there was Bernice, a nurse who would often skip her shifts, leaving the charge nurse to cover for her. Even when present, Bernice was hard to find. Despite her name listed as my sister's assigned nurse, she'd claim that another nurse, Drexel, was in charge. This was clearly a staffing issue.

Some nurses had a disconcerting habit of questioning my concerns and observations, which felt like gaslighting. This unprofessional behavior caused significant stress and frustration.

Despite these disappointments, I want to recognize a few individuals who stood out for their compassion. Joyce, Alice,
Naomi, Brianna, Connie, Terry, and Gloria were rays of hope, offering kindness and understanding during this trying period. Their efforts did not go unnoticed.

Sadly, this lack of empathy wasn't restricted to the lower ranks. The charge nurse, Kevin, often came across as unsympathetic and impatient, and his suggestions felt irrelevant and dismissive.

Dr. Rahman, my sister's assigned doctor, was a particular letdown. His brief "check-ins" involved a hasty "she's okay" before he dashed out of the room, leaving no space for me to discuss my sister's condition or ask questions. It was only when I made significant efforts to 'catch' him that he would engage in a more meaningful conversation. It's disheartening to think family members must strive so hard for attention during such difficult times. I understand that everyone's busy, but the evident understaffing at this facility led to inadequate patient monitoring. This lapse in staffing has resulted in my sister returning to the ER, facing another potential concussion.

On a brighter note, there were other clinicians who were more engaged and informative than Dr. Rahman. Their input was invaluable, and I'm grateful for it.

In conclusion, while there were a few kind-hearted individuals at Horizon, the overall experience was far from satisfactory. I strongly urge the management to take this feedback to heart and make necessary changes to enhance patient care and communication.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Georgina Sylvia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIZIBX465faOr_0NLnrLIlGxZyaPjZpxWtiRBpcz8E-Lm1h5Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is one of those don’t judge a book by it’s cover. The outside doesn’t look like much but the nurses that work there are truly wonderful. My husband was there for an entire month. Wayne and Joellen took super care of him and answered all of my medical questions. I spent almost all of every day there and they made me feel welcomed as well. Not their job to make sure I was comfortable but they did. You both are Rockstars.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Traci Devine","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXLJfxQikJZJuw7AKN5znAtwBvGniQ7Udjpk_qaRkm2-snVknna=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"My sister was there for 9 days and it was hell for her. Her hair was so matted from not brushing it. She couldn't do it, I was not allowed to go and do it. When I asked about they said they would have to cut it. No.... that's just laziness. Conditioner, comb and patience is all it took. She didn't like their food so their attitude was if you don't like it than you don't eat. I asked if I could bring.her soup that she likes and no one bothered to answer me back. She lost so much weight there. I took her out AMA before they had a chance to neglect her more. There's so much more but I'll stop.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Nicole Smith","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLqEGNXI6TwQ2yqpaBWeWOpUbzeH268jy-JN7T1-Fboz_EcCPzN=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently visited Horizon and was very impressed. Nothing, for 4 days and night, they just stood there like robots not doing anything good for the healthcare industry.

Today, Nov.29, I came back because of too much pain from my incisions. One nurse by the name of Kiara at the ER was very Compassionate but unfortunately once I got to my designated room, the nurse assigned to me by the name of Len really believes that he is better than a doctor and is trying his best to change the schedule of when I am supposed to take my pain meds. IS THAT EVEN MORALLY CORRECT????
Update:
I’m on day 3 of my confinement here at Sunrise hospital and it’s very unfortunate that I am still suffering from so much pain just because most of the nurses lack compassion and is not doing their job well. They move slowly as if they really hate their jobs and it’s very obvious that they don’t really care about what their patients are going through. I even missed two doses that’s why my pain level went back up to 10 but I didn’t hear even a small apology from her. These people SHOULD NOT BE WORKING as nurses because helpless patients like me who are in so much pain suffer unnecessarily. I’m currently at room 3032 and I am welcome to be interviewed regarding my complaints for this is a serious issue. These outrageous practices by these horrible nurses has to stop and I pray that the people who manages them do something about it. There is so much suffering in this world that can be alleviated by mere compassion for others. May God help us.

Conclusion of my entire hospital stay from Nov.29- Dec.3.
I was surprised to get a visit from Ms Mary Osungwu, the Manager of Medical Surgical Unit who wanted to discuss with me the feedback that I wrote here. It somehow relieved me that they seem to be taking these things seriously and is promising to make some changes to improve their services especially with the nurses performance. You are seen by a nurse practitioner instead of a doctor. They take you quickly for a CT scan. After that, a nurse came to discuss a balloon sinuplasty procedure, which she strongly encouraged you to consider. Pressure! Ask for a second opinion. Subsequently, they billed my insurance nearly $2,000, including charges for a nasal endoscopy that was never done. In total they charged $559 for the visit without seeing a doctor, $884 for the nonexistent nasal endoscopy, and $500 for the CT scan. So, just a heads-up… be careful!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Nazim Chambi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUqL48T47vd5opSAw_0or0hesJ5l0rNeZGpy9rmvBSJeKWfwxEk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"After 4 years of sinus issues, no taste, no smell. It was pretty a bad issue.  I called ENT after ENT and none were able to see me, unless it was 6-8 months out. Some even rude! I almost gave up until I found Dr Nalbone’s office and team.
Was seen within a week and  I had a CT scan done and saw how serious the blockage was: Polyps, swelling, and a deviated septum.  Dr Nalbone, told me we needed the Balloon Sinuplasty surgery done (we tried steroids, and other treatments first).
The procedure was done and wow!! I can breath, smell, taste, sleep better. They did wonderful work. Jaclyn PA has been very helpful with my recovery and follow ups. I recommend this office as other ENT’s were really hard to book an appointment unless it was 6 months out.  My experience here has been lovely, all the staff is wonderful and understanding. Thank you for helping me breath better, and for always making me feel welcome.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipPBz0G21f66o2bdcwkgX_qWMq7IOLVF0FZxtNMF&fid=0x0:0xdca79587ef05d62"],"When":"2024-2-6"},{"Name":"Natalie DeNardo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIbFIgGTNvQaaQdYeSOwHmT9vVqcGBCDUvSmZR4dkF8jcXgFg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"What a fantastic experience. They are one time, efficient and so nice. I seen Jaclyn Crame on my first visit and what a wonderful person. She took the time to
Explain everything to me and recommend that I have a low invasive procedure. Best decision ever . I’m two weeks post op and I can breathe better. Sinus pain is gone, throat pain gone, post nasal drip gone. The list goes on and on. I highly recommend this place and if you are having sinus issues look no further. This is office class act through and through.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Brenda Cunningham","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW0N4VbciZTHdAZWsjXlgyWauA7QkTNQnsf4gUyW9hhF_p6PqMo7g=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I went to this Dr because I kept having nosebleeds and problems with my ears when I fly. The first thing they did was a CT scan of my sinuses.  When they showed me the results it was clear that I had lots of inflammation, deviated septum And blockages in the eustachian tubs in my ears. They recommended that I have a balloon sinuplasty which could be done in their office.   When they said it would be done without anesthesia I began to panic. Ellen took the time to answer all of my questions and assure me that I would be numbed and relaxed with a sleeping pill so I would be comfortable during the procedure. She even offered to have someone hold my hand if I wanted. My recovery after the procedure was easy and not uncomfortable. I followed their instructions and went for my followup with Jaclyn Crame today. I no longer have the blockage in my ears, my sinuses are no longer inflamed and I can breathe out of both nostrils.  This turned out to be an easy fix for my issues!  Everyone in this office is very kind and friendly.  Thank you for taking care of my issues!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alexander LeVeque","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKzqdFEPqvlgtsa-0Vh3pUco5OZ4JmAr2coM5lN73rP5L_IhA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I have known Dr. Nalbone for years. I had horrible post nasal drip for months before making an appointment.  Was a bit nervous but after the surgery and a few days of healing my breathing and post nasal drip issues have drastically improved!
The staff was very friendly and helpful and all questions were answered before and after my procedure.

If you want to breathe again I would highly recommend this place!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Rex Roscoe Roggasch Jr (R Cubed)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU4s36c5ulG5ALFUaM1G0HZL0Slk027ylz1HpFcbCp_vuRsCF6Z=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We, my wife and I, want to share with anyone that’s been experiencing sinus and throat congestion with zero effective results. My wife has been to many allergy, respiratory specialists as well as her regular doctors over 15 years to address her sinus and throat coughing and congestion only to be prescribed many pills, inhalers and breathing medical devices. She even went to Peru to a specialist in a Japanese Clinic, with same failed result. All had CT scans and endoscopy and all followed the same failed prescriptions.

At wits ends, my wife had discovered Vegas Breathe free, sinus and allergy center. I was skeptical after the thousands of dollars previously spent! I am, we are so happy we went!

The clinic, the staff and facility are 5 star, friendly professionals in a very clean environment. Queen and our PA, Jaclyn with Caroline went above and beyond to exam my wife, explain and answer every single question i skeptically had. They quickly identified what had historically affecting my wife. It was mechanical and no pharmaceutical would ever relieve my wife’s condition! The procedure is called ballon sinuplasty.
I cannot say enough about PA Jaclyn, Queen,Caroline, Aya, the entire procedure team and Dr Vincent Nalbone!!!!
My wife’s throat issue completely went away within 24 hours!!!

If you’ve tried everything else and are still struggling with a throat wheeze and nasal congestion, do yourself and family and go to Vegas Breathe Free Sinus and Allergy center.
Say hello for us, Juana and Rex!
Peace","Images":null,"When":""}]

There were some things administered that we were not consulted with and, we trusted the acumen of the doctors to make sure mom was never in pain. Mom had many tests done and it took several days to really determine the extent of her condition. Bottom line, if anything were to happen to me or family members, I trust Centennial hospital to treat and care for us. I was there for pre operation tests, every nurse and technician were very nice and compassionate , that help a lot since I’m feeling nervous about the surgery. The only reason I don’t give them a 5 star is because the Team that supposed to gave me a explanation of my surgery weren’t there because of the holidays they said, so they gave me a book instead and let me watch a video. Other than that everything was great. Thank you Spring Valley Hospital.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kathy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVT5P5OgIJ-dmN8D5VeSOh9vUWSaKv6lLsCbz941URyJZi-wYg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently visited Spring Valley Hospital for an unexpected emergency and overall, my experience was positive. The staff, including nurses, doctors, and support personnel, were professional, friendly, attentive and kind. I felt well taken care of throughout my visit. The medical team took the time to answer my questions and explained things clearly, which made me feel more comfortable. The hospital itself was clean, well-maintained and the equipment and technology appeared to be up-to-date, which reassured me about the level of care.My treatment was effective and thorough, and I was highly satisfied with the care provided. I’m confident in the care I received and feel that the hospital staff did their best to address my needs. I highly recommend, if the need arises,","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mami Mayans","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWucm8cuRFB2cStdQebhU8ullsrMa8gxfUdtgxeLop3tVswtYc=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I had an exceptional experience at Spring Valley Hospital. From my pre-op up to the moment I arrived for my surgery, I was impressed by the patience and empathy of everyone I interacted with. It was clear that the staff genuinely cared about my well-being. I always felt well-informed and welcomed, thanks to the excellent communication from the team.

After my surgery, Nurse Loren from recovery took extraordinary care of me. She made me feel completely at ease, and I trusted her completely. Honestly, the comfort and attention I received from Nurse Loren were unlike anything I've ever experienced at any other hospital—I've had my share of hospital stays, but none have compared to the level of care I received here.

Thank you, Spring Valley Hospital, and a special thank you to Nurse Loren for your unwavering patience, professionalism, and empathy. God bless all of you..❤️👍","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chris Knox","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV9OtFHdWLv3_K_tmYIF4YlD5owpB_RzJ80DRF8D95bQxcxs2c=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"For a troubling time, the staff and doctors were very courteous and supportive. I received top notch medical care during my brief stay.
The treatment information was very well presented and reassuring. The treatment itself was executed extremely well and very timely.
Thankfully, we had Blue Cross travel insurance, which their team dealt with promptly and without delay.
They had me back on my feet in a very reasonable amount of time and my wife and I were able to continue enjoying our vacation.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"The Ferndinos","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWyUx0gVJmYJEg6o3zyzkDXLpqRSHpKei6AppTYjgj9MvWF2FU=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"I want to start this review by saying I had a c section at spring valley hospital that ended up being a week earlier than expected and my labor nurse and Dr. Nguyen and her team were amazing. While almost all of the staff we interacted with at the hospital were really great, our biggest complaint was my day shift nurse Lita. For starters, and for me most importantly, I felt like I was getting pressured very heavily to take narcotics post c section which I made very clear to my night nurse and my Dr. that I didn’t want to take due to history of addiction in my family and I wasn’t comfortable. I expressed I was managing my pain with the other non-narcotic medication they were giving me, and my pain never exceeded a 7/10 during my stay, and that was my first night right after the c section. Even after telling this day shift nurse several times that I didn’t want to take narcotics, she still tried getting me to take this medication by asking me several times throughout her shift, even after I expressed that my pain had gone down to a 3 or 4/10. Secondly, both my husband and I felt like every piece of information we got from her seemed to contradict information we were given from other staff. For example, we were told that someone was going to come speak with us to do the paperwork for our daughters birth certificate. Only a few hours from being discharged, still no one had come to our room and when we asked the nurse when someone would be coming by she said no one and we fill the paperwork out ourselves. She also said we would receive an email from the health district when it was ready, which also was incorrect. Lastly, she never would address my husband directly when coming into the room and completely ignored him regarding every test or question they were running or asking about our daughter. She was the only staff member that we interacted with that treated him this way. Overall I would still be very happy to give birth again at this hospital. I would just hope to not experience working with another nurse like this that added lots confusion and frustration to our time there.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Mary Dela Cruz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL-Iee2o9i1lYVamvMb-mHV8KR6PRNiFpP2HO3hk-Xt74umSw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The most amazing and caring health care providers I’ve ever met.  Very attentive detailed, organized and professional staff. Special thanks for the service and care I received from Nurse Marly and Nurse Amy. And the other two Asian nurses I didn’t get a chance to get their names. And made sure to recheck my vitals before discharge. Thank you so greatly for your help. 6/21 and 6/22  during early morning shifts of 9am. Will be visiting with SPRING VALLEY HOSPITAL ER again for any of my emergency care needs. Thank you 😊","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sAF1QipPpH2I9SQkKjDpNV6Bnn9dUufLJdmsUpr_K6u-n&fid=0x0:0x4c4797e107911712"],"When":"2024-6-23"}]
